when matt murdock was a kid, he lost his sight
in an accident involving radioactive chemicals.
though he could no longer see, the chemicals
heightened murdock’s other senses and imbued
him with an amazing 360-degree radar sense.

after a battle against the hand that literally
spanned life and death, matt was returned to the
living world but left missing many of his memories.

the cost of matt's trip to and from the afterlife
was quickly laid bare: portions of his soul,
infected by the seven deadly sins, have possessed
those closest to matt--including kingpin--and
he is the only one who can free them!

meanwhile, matt's battles with his demons have
imperiled his refuge at saint nicholas’ youth
home, where he's been working as a priest since
his reawakening. one of the youngsters, jason, has
discovered matt's secret identity and, in his anger,
run away. matt has enlisted elektra’s help to track

jason down--but first, they must deal with
kingpin who has been possessed

when matt murdock was a kid, he lost his sight in
an accident involving radioactive chemicals. though
he could no longer see, the chemicals heightened
murdock’s other senses and imbued him with an
amazing 360-degree radar sense.

after a battle against the hand, matt's soul was
fractured, infected by the seven deadly sins and
possessed those closest to him. matt has exorcized
five of the devils from his enemies and allies alike,
leaving just two: pride and wrath.

but the toll on matt's loved ones and his once
unshakable faith has been heavy as, in an act of
supreme rejection, guilt and anger, he renounced
his faith and his god. and the hits keep coming
as matt received a legal summons from his old
partner, foggy nelson, threatening the church
and youth home that matt has been working at,
saint nicholas’. this new threat has thrown saint
nicholas’ into even more disarray, with tensions
already fraught after wrath attacked father javi
and seemingly kidnapped one of the youths in the
home’s care, jason.

seeking to clear his mind, matt patrolled the city
as daredevil, only to stumble upon a horrifying
discovery: bullseye’s severed arm. but before he
could investigate further, daredevil was attacked

by wrath and its vessel's identity was
revealed to be none other than

None_page_Punisher_-_Red_Band__0038.jpg:
here we go!

hello, punisher readers!
editor devin lewis reporting
to you live and in-print from
marvel hq in the heart of
new york! as i type this, it’s a
sweltering summer here in the
city—and it isn’t much easier
for those folks in the marvel
universe, as things are really
starting to heat up with the
return of the one and only
frank castle!

i'll level with you readers out
there in mighty marveldom—
i’ve always been a pretty
big punisher fan. | was first
introduced to the character in
1994’s spider-man animated
series, and even as a tyke,
there was something about
frank castle’s look, no-
nonsense approach and
explosive ordnance that
made him undeniably cool. in
hindsight, it might have also
helped that | was 5 years old
and the cartoon incarnation
of the punisher wore his
classic white-boot-white-glove
costume, a war journal
inspired bandana and a trench
coat from the pages of war
zone. truly, it was the best
of all worlds—and with a true
wealth of works from too many
legendary creators to list, the
team on the cartoon certainly
had a lot to work with.

no matter the reason, | was
hooked, and it didn’t take long
for me to start adding the
punisher to both my pull list
and the titles i’d keep an eye
out for while back-issue-bin
diving. from “return to big
nothing” to “franken-castle”
and everything in-between, i’ve
always kept an eye on frank’s
adventures, and so to be able

to work on this incarnation of
the character is as close to a
dream come true as one gets.

but i’m not the only punisher
fan working on this series!
heck, i’m probably not even
the biggest punisher fan!
that title almost surely belongs
to series scribe ben percy,
who, wouldn’t you know, also
has an affection for the marvel
u’s most vindictively violent
vigilante. but don’t take my
word for it! from ben himself:

when | was a kid, we moved
around a lot, so | was always
in a new school, a new town, a
new address—but the stories |
surrounded myself with stayed
the same, and the punisher
was a constant. i’d read issues
of war journal over and over
until they fell apart in my hands.
i’d fish issues off the spinner
rack at the gas station, and i’d
hunt them out of long boxes at
garage sales. why | gravitated so
strongly toward a dark-hearted
vengeance machine like frank
castle, | don’t know. there’s
probably something wrong with
my hard wiring. but | suspect it
had something to do with forever
looking out of a different window.

when you’re always the new
kid, you need to know how to
fight. when you’re always the
new kid, you’re mourning what’s
been lost and left behind. when
you’re always the new kid, you
have to figure out your own
@#$%. an antihero like frank
was nomadic but at home with
himself: completely capable and
in charge of his own narrative and
defined by a resolve and code
(no matter how problematic)
that | loved. there was a kinship
there with conan and wolverine,
certainly—ghost rider too—

email us ar
mheroesomarvel.com
and mark your

messages "okay to
print” for a chance
to see them answered
in future (ssues/

but also characters like roland
deschain, the titular gunslinger
from stephen king’s dark tower
series (who | also gravitated
toward). these antiheroes were
driven to battle back against the
darkness of the world. monsters
fighting monsters.

so writing this series means a lot
to me, and i’m thrilled to be paired
with julius ohta, who absolutely
killed it (literally) when we joined
forces on hellverine. we’re
going to stay true to legacy but
hopefully put our own unique
stamp on the franchise. yes,
the punisher is defined by grief
and grit, mayhemic action set
pieces, doom and steel-cold
justice—we’ll be serving up
plenty of that—but we’re going
to open this run in the style of a
paranoid thriller.

the punisher is aman on the run
who has lost his memory and
internal compass. he barely
knows who he is—beyond his
reptilian self. he only knows
he must survive in a world that
wants to use him as a weapon or
see him buried for good.

frank is back...in black. lock and
load. thanks for giving us a shot.

unlike frank castle, though,
ben’s not in this alone! he’s
been joined by his hellverine
collaborator julius ohta, who’s
been doing the work of his
career on this series! |
can tell you from firsthand
experience that julius has sunk
a lot of blood, sweat and tears
into this series, and it shows on
each and every page.

it’s an honor to be working on
punisher alongside ben percy!

there was a great synergy from
the very first conversations
about how to portray this iconic

continued on next page
None_page_Punisher_-_Red_Band__0039.jpg:
character, and | felt very excited
about what we were about to do!

when | learned it would be
red band, | was even more
excited and felt in my comfort
zone, with the freedom to bring
graphic scenes and the violence
characteristic of the punisher.

| hope i can do the best work
possible, one that honors the
character’s legacy and that the
fans will love!

the story that ben and julius
have in store for you is as
intense and adventurous a tale
as frank’s ever been in before.
iv’ll see him squaring up with

foes he’s, literally, only seen
in his worst nightmares and
facing down situations similar
to those that super heroes like
spider-man and wolverine
have dealt with before—but
never frank castle.

the punisher isn’t a hero, and
he wouldn’t call himself one.
his responses to those foes
and situations will be in-kind.
so brace yourself, readers.
it’s going to be a bumpy and
bloody ride.

until then, though, we want
to hear from you! what did
you think of this first issue?

on sale 10.15.25

let us know by writing to
mheroes@marvel.com!
mark your missive “okay to
print” and it may see publication
in a future installment of this
very column!

and until then, be sure to tell
your comic shop that you
want the best—and the best
is punisher!

next issue:
frankie gets his gun!

stay sharp!
devin

marvel pauses to remember sal buscema, a
foundational marvel artist since 1969. buscema
was a prolific penciler and inker, working on
nearly every marvel character. his clear, dynamic
storytelling was a feature of long, historic runs
on spectacular spider-man, incredible hulk
and rom. buscema was famed for drawing
bombastic roundhouse punches that sent
characters sprawling across the page, but he
could also be capable of great subtlety and
emotion in his character acting. “our pal sal” |.
will be missed by generations of marvel fans.

daredevil_page_Daredevil_012_(2024)_0003:
when matt murdock was a kid, he lost his sight
in an accident involving radioactive chemicals.
though he could no longer see, the chemicals
heightened murdock’s other senses and imbued
him with an amazing 360-degree radar sense.

after a battle against the hand that literally
spanned life and death, matt was returned to the
living world but left missing many of his memories.

the cost of matt's trip to and from the afterlife
was quickly laid bare: portions of his soul,
infected by the seven deadly sins, have possessed
those closest to matt--including kingpin--and
he is the only one who can free them!

meanwhile, matt's battles with his demons have
imperiled his refuge at saint nicholas’ youth
home, where he's been working as a priest since
his reawakening. one of the youngsters, jason, has
discovered matt's secret identity and, in his anger,
run away. matt has enlisted elektra’s help to track

jason down--but first, they must deal with
kingpin who has been possessed
by matt's own wd

daredevil_page_Daredevil_017_(2025)_0003:
when matt murdock was a kid, he lost his sight in
an accident involving radioactive chemicals. though
he could no longer see, the chemicals heightened
murdock’s other senses and imbued him with an
amazing 360-degree radar sense.

after a battle against the hand, matt's soul was
fractured, infected by the seven deadly sins and
possessed those closest to him. matt has exorcized
five of the devils from his enemies and allies alike,
leaving just two: pride and wrath.

but the toll on matt's loved ones and his once
unshakable faith has been heavy as, in an act of
supreme rejection, guilt and anger, he renounced
his faith and his god. and the hits keep coming
as matt received a legal summons from his old
partner, foggy nelson, threatening the church
and youth home that matt has been working at,
saint nicholas’. this new threat has thrown saint
nicholas’ into even more disarray, with tensions
already fraught after wrath attacked father javi
and seemingly kidnapped one of the youths in the
home’s care, jason.

seeking to clear his mind, matt patrolled the city
as daredevil, only to stumble upon a horrifying
discovery: bullseye’s severed arm. but before he
could investigate further, daredevil was attacked

by wrath and its vessel's identity was
revealed to be none other than
his missing wd

here we go!

hello, punisher readers!
editor devin lewis reporting
to you live and in-print from
marvel hq in the heart of
new york! as i type this, it’s a
sweltering summer here in the
city—and it isn’t much easier
for those folks in the marvel
universe, as things are really
starting to heat up with the
return of the one and only
frank castle!

i'll level with you readers out
there in mighty marveldom—
i’ve always been a pretty
big punisher fan. | was first
introduced to the character in
1994’s spider-man animated
series, and even as a tyke,
there was something about
frank castle’s look, no-
nonsense approach and
explosive ordnance that
made him undeniably cool. in
hindsight, it might have also
helped that | was 5 years old
and the cartoon incarnation
of the punisher wore his
classic white-boot-white-glove
costume, a war journal
inspired bandana and a trench
coat from the pages of war
zone. truly, it was the best
of all worlds—and with a true
wealth of works from too many
legendary creators to list, the
team on the cartoon certainly
had a lot to work with.

no matter the reason, | was
hooked, and it didn’t take long
for me to start adding the
punisher to both my pull list
and the titles i’d keep an eye
out for while back-issue-bin
diving. from “return to big
nothing” to “franken-castle”
and everything in-between, i’ve
always kept an eye on frank’s
adventures, and so to be able

to work on this incarnation of
the character is as close to a
dream come true as one gets.

but i’m not the only punisher
fan working on this series!
heck, i’m probably not even
the biggest punisher fan!
that title almost surely belongs
to series scribe ben percy,
who, wouldn’t you know, also
has an affection for the marvel
u’s most vindictively violent
vigilante. but don’t take my
word for it! from ben himself:

when | was a kid, we moved
around a lot, so | was always
in a new school, a new town, a
new address—but the stories |
surrounded myself with stayed
the same, and the punisher
was a constant. i’d read issues
of war journal over and over
until they fell apart in my hands.
i’d fish issues off the spinner
rack at the gas station, and i’d
hunt them out of long boxes at
garage sales. why | gravitated so
strongly toward a dark-hearted
vengeance machine like frank
castle, | don’t know. there’s
probably something wrong with
my hard wiring. but | suspect it
had something to do with forever
looking out of a different window.

when you’re always the new
kid, you need to know how to
fight. when you’re always the
new kid, you’re mourning what’s
been lost and left behind. when
you’re always the new kid, you
have to figure out your own
@#$%. an antihero like frank
was nomadic but at home with
himself: completely capable and
in charge of his own narrative and
defined by a resolve and code
(no matter how problematic)
that | loved. there was a kinship
there with conan and wolverine,
certainly—ghost rider too—

email us ar
mheroesomarvel.com
and mark your

messages "okay to
print” for a chance
to see them answered
in future (ssues/

but also characters like roland
deschain, the titular gunslinger
from stephen king’s dark tower
series (who | also gravitated
toward). these antiheroes were
driven to battle back against the
darkness of the world. monsters
fighting monsters.

so writing this series means a lot
to me, and i’m thrilled to be paired
with julius ohta, who absolutely
killed it (literally) when we joined
forces on hellverine. we’re
going to stay true to legacy but
hopefully put our own unique
stamp on the franchise. yes,
the punisher is defined by grief
and grit, mayhemic action set
pieces, doom and steel-cold
justice—we’ll be serving up
plenty of that—but we’re going
to open this run in the style of a
paranoid thriller.

the punisher is aman on the run
who has lost his memory and
internal compass. he barely
knows who he is—beyond his
reptilian self. he only knows
he must survive in a world that
wants to use him as a weapon or
see him buried for good.

frank is back...in black. lock and
load. thanks for giving us a shot.

unlike frank castle, though,
ben’s not in this alone! he’s
been joined by his hellverine
collaborator julius ohta, who’s
been doing the work of his
career on this series! |
can tell you from firsthand
experience that julius has sunk
a lot of blood, sweat and tears
into this series, and it shows on
each and every page.

it’s an honor to be working on
punisher alongside ben percy!

there was a great synergy from
the very first conversations
about how to portray this iconic

continued on next page
character, and | felt very excited
about what we were about to do!

when | learned it would be
red band, | was even more
excited and felt in my comfort
zone, with the freedom to bring
graphic scenes and the violence
characteristic of the punisher.

| hope i can do the best work
possible, one that honors the
character’s legacy and that the
fans will love!

the story that ben and julius
have in store for you is as
intense and adventurous a tale
as frank’s ever been in before.
iv’ll see him squaring up with

foes he’s, literally, only seen
in his worst nightmares and
facing down situations similar
to those that super heroes like
spider-man and wolverine
have dealt with before—but
never frank castle.

the punisher isn’t a hero, and
he wouldn’t call himself one.
his responses to those foes
and situations will be in-kind.
so brace yourself, readers.

marvel pauses to remember sal buscema, a
foundational marvel artist since 1969. buscema
was a prolific penciler and inker, working on
nearly every marvel character. his clear, dynamic
storytelling was a feature of long, historic runs
on spectacular spider-man, incredible hulk
and rom. buscema was famed for drawing
bombastic roundhouse punches that sent
characters sprawling across the page, but he
could also be capable of great subtlety and
emotion in his character acting. “our pal sal” |.
will be missed by generations of marvel fans.
